description: How the child elements of a Block are grouped (§5.4). Spec canon: superset, giant_set, circuit, drop_set. Extended with the intra-set-rest and contrast families that incumbents (incl. Hevy) under-model — rest_pause is the founder's motivating gap (METHODOLOGY-VALIDATION, Hevy test).

tokens.label: Superset
tokens.description: Two exercises performed back-to-back with no rest between.

tokens.label: Giant Set
tokens.description: Three or more exercises performed in sequence with no rest.

tokens.label: Circuit
tokens.description: A rotation through several stations, typically minimal rest.

tokens.label: Drop Set
tokens.description: Canonical encoding: post-first WorkUnits carry setRole 'drop' with reduced load and no rest (§5.4).

tokens.label: Rest-Pause
tokens.description: One effort to near-failure, then short (~10-20 s) rests with more reps; child WorkUnits carry setRole 'rest_pause'/'cluster' and a short rest.

tokens.label: Cluster Set
tokens.description: Intra-set rests at fixed points (e.g. 5×(2 reps, 10 s)); child WorkUnits carry setRole 'cluster'.

tokens.label: Complex
tokens.description: Several movements performed in sequence without setting the implement down (e.g. a barbell complex); child WorkUnits each carry their own exerciseRef.

tokens.label: Contrast / PAP
tokens.description: Heavy strength paired with explosive/plyometric work to exploit potentiation.

tokens.label: Mechanical Drop Set
tokens.description: Switch to a mechanically easier variation (angle/grip/exercise) to continue past failure; each child WorkUnit carries its own exerciseRef.

tokens.label: Pre-Exhaust
tokens.description: An isolation movement before (or after) a compound to target a muscle.
